alleges was fired for complaining about the unlawful pay practice.

original text:
Businesses use shared services streamline activities, concentrating transactional activities into centralised function.
This model can reduce costs and increase the efficiency processes within the business, whilst freeing time for greater focus strategy.
Shared Service functions can add lot value, but you get wrong can have huge consequence.
Employee experience ruined and relationships throughout the business with can damaged.
But why does fail?
When you implement Shared Service Centre (HRSSC), there are two groups people who need communicated with and engaged: the people working there and those who will using it.
Both these groups are equally important.
You need take your customers the journey with you, they understand how and why you’re changing things.
either these groups isn’t engaged, the HRSSC simply won’t work.
crucial establish the right metrics analyse.
This allows you monitor data see how your teams are performing and highlight current inefficiencies, well potential problem areas that may need investigation.
Measuring results and data enables informed decisions made that drive your HRSSC continually develop and run better.
This gives your teams the resources they need successful, provides employees with better experience and ultimately gets the business results you want.
Delivering HRSSC into business takes time.
isn’t something you can decide and then implement within weeks and you can’t half-heartedly.
There are lot things consider from mapping out processes and ensuring you have the right technology, right down hiring and onboarding the right talent.
you rush any areas don’t give them the focus they need, the chances are they will fail.
Having the right leader important for any team, but particularly shared service environment.
The wrong leader can create unhappy, disengaged team who lose their passion for delivering excellence.
When this happens, the knock-on effect across the business can immense.
good shared service leader should able look beyond the HRSSC and take into account the impact has employees, well customers and clients.
Technology fundamental component any HRSSC without the right technology, just won’t work.
It’s really important that you check your current system fit for purpose and it's not, fix it!
tech big investment, make sure you choose the right one.
Meet multiple vendors, get demonstrations and challenge them, make sure the system does everything you need to.
Modern technology allows manage incoming requests, review case histories and related employee files, provide consistent responses and escalate case when necessary.
You would amazed how often people miss out one the key elements ensure their Shared Service Centre success.
Make sure you’re not one them!
For all things, Shared Services, change and transformation and you would like feature our ‘Insiders Story’ blog, email kate@refind.co.uk.
You can view more about Kate Wass our Shared Services specialist here.
Why won't top performing shared service professionals join your business?
And what about it.
Download our free eBook here.
Recruiting the best talent Shared Services, Change Transformation.
What are the three keys scaling up?

also produces log file the log directory.

The new version includes many new exciting functions (RBL, RWL, Automatic white listing) and gives you very granular control over how the filter works.
And it’s still available free version!
and have look!.
Since have had lot problems with false positives with the black lists that I’m using Exchange <hide> server started looking into another way filtering spam.
The obvious choice additional protection fell greylisting you can read more about what here
The problem with this that there doesn’t seem any free products out there for Exchange and don’t want set Linux box yet another box the rack decided write one myself.
Usually receive <hide>-<hide> spam attempts per day that means that mails day are slipping trough.
These get matched blacklist that not that aggressive and the result this that spam level has gone down almost while haven’t had single false positive yet.
About the program.
consists two parts.
Greylist installs .dll and connects the SMTP service’s OnInboundCommand RCPT.
reads configuration from Greylist.cfg and uses Greylist.mdb for logging entries.
also produces log file the log directory.
Greylist admin creates and configures the above files well controls the settings and the white list.
Continue blocking for minutes.
Block Source IP, Sender email address, Recipient address all together any combination.
White list (always allow) Source IP, Sender email address, Recipient address any combination.
Clean out entries older then days the first session the day.
Stores data Microsoft access database, .mdb MsSQL db.
Configures: Block for minutes, Max age days, White list.
Configures which items use when blocking Source IP, Sender email address, Recipient address all together any combination.
Displays blocked items and passed items totals.
Displays current items database.
Displays block rate according all entries the database.
Registered users can now set how Greylist will behave blocked item.
can either send the <hide> message and then disconnect (default) keep the session alive and accept more recipients.
Registered users can now change the message Greylist sends out.
This especially great for corporate usage where each company can have custom message.
For the rest Greylist stays the same for unregistered users.
Added new tab with controls for Disconnect <hide> and <hide> message.

Wild week the Shore Conference St. John Vianney took Rumson, Lenape and Holmdel and won all three contests.
saw two undefeated division teams get knocked off when Southern lost Toms River North and Manchester lost Point Boro which opened the race now for both divisions.
Neptune, St. Rose, Middletown South and Vianney all moved one game closer taking their division crowns.
With just two weeks remaining before the Shore Conference Tournament selection, total teams are above .<hide> and five more are looking get the .<hide> mark qualify for the tournament.
Standings are based stats and scores reported Shore Sports Network coaches/team representatives.
Coaches may email corrections brauntenj@gmail.com.
Standings are based games through Friday January <hide>th, <hide>.
